summary: Legacy validated record without machine-checkable validation proof. Official Windows service control for Connected User Experiences and Telemetry (DiagTrack). Microsoft documents the service name, default start mode, and says it is OK to disable on Windows IoT Enterprise. The app disables the service by setting its start mode to Disabled.

setting.name: Connected User Experiences and Telemetry Service
setting.casual_explanation: This service handles Windows telemetry and connected-user-experience data collection. Disabling it reduces diagnostic and usage reporting, but some connected-experience features can lose functionality.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it when they want less telemetry collection or a more stripped-down Windows configuration.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling the service reduces telemetry collection, but it can also limit connected experiences and telemetry-dependent diagnostics.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Windows IoT Enterprise service guidance lists Connected User Experiences and Telemetry `(DiagTrack)` with a default start mode of `Automatic` and marks it `OK to disable`. Microsoft describes the service as managing event-driven collection and transmission of diagnostic and usage information for connected-user-experience features. The app uses a service start-mode tweak that disables `DiagTrack`.

allowed_values.label: Windows default
allowed_values.meaning: Telemetry and connected-user-experience collection stay available with the documented default start mode.

allowed_values.label: Telemetry service disabled
allowed_values.meaning: DiagTrack no longer starts and the documented telemetry-related service behavior is turned off.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app disables the DiagTrack service by setting its start mode to Disabled.

windows_defaults.label: Microsoft documented default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using the normal telemetry service configuration

states.rationale: Microsoft lists DiagTrack with an Automatic default start mode in the service guidance.

windows_defaults.notes: This baseline preserves the documented telemetry-service behavior.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that should keep normal connected-experience behavior
Users who want Windows diagnostics available
avoid_for: Users who intentionally want the telemetry service disabled

states.rationale: Keep the documented default service mode.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal telemetry-service behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You intentionally want to turn off the service.
tradeoffs: The service continues to manage diagnostic and usage transmission.

recommended_profiles.label: Reduced telemetry service
avoid_for: Systems that need Windows connected-experience diagnostics intact
recommended_profiles.good_when: You intentionally want the telemetry service turned off.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You rely on connected-experience or telemetry-dependent diagnostics.
tradeoffs: Telemetry service activity is reduced by disabling DiagTrack.
Some connected-user-experience features can lose functionality.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Guidance on configuring system services - Connected User Experiences and Telemetry
evidence.summary: Microsoft lists Connected User Experiences and Telemetry `(DiagTrack)` with start mode `Automatic` and marks it `OK to disable`, describing its telemetry and connected-experience role.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider disables the `DiagTrack` service through the batch service start-mode tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the DiagTrack service name, its Automatic default start mode, the telemetry behavior it provides, and that it is OK to disable in the service guidance used here. The app's service-start-mode action matches that documented control surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Connected User Experiences and Telemetry (DiagTrack); Automatic; DiagTrack enables features that supports in-application and connected user experiences and manages the event-driven collection and transmission of diagnostic and usage information...; OK to disable: This service provides functionality that is useful to some but not all enterprises, and security-focused enterprises that don't use it can safely disable it.
validation_proof.notes: The Microsoft services guidance page explicitly lists the DiagTrack service, its Automatic baseline, its telemetry role, and that it is OK to disable.
